title: Watermelon
statement: Given an integer w (1 <= w <= 100), print YES if w can be split into two positive even integers, otherwise print NO.
output: Print YES if w can be split into two positive even integers, otherwise print NO.

title: Way Too Long Words
statement: Input starts with integer n, followed by n words. For each word with length > 10, print first letter + (len-2) + last letter; otherwise print the word unchanged.
input: The first line contains integer n, then n lines each with a word.
output: For each word, print the shortened form if length > 10; otherwise print the word.

title: Team
statement: First line n, then n lines each have three 0/1 values. Count how many problems have at least two 1s.
input: First line n. Next n lines contain three integers (0 or 1).
output: Number of problems that the team will solve.

title: Next Round
statement: Given n, k and scores in non-increasing order, count participants with positive score and score >= score[k].
input: First line n and k. Second line has n integers (scores).

title: Domino Piling
statement: Given board m x n, output maximum number of 2x1 dominoes that fit without overlap.

title: Bit++
statement: Start x=0. Given n statements from {X++, ++X, X--, --X}, apply them and output final x.
input: First line n. Next n lines each contain one statement.

title: Beautiful Matrix
statement: In a 5x5 matrix with one cell equal to 1, output moves needed to bring it to center (3,3) using swaps with adjacent rows/cols.
input: Five lines each with five integers (0 or 1). Exactly one cell is 1.

title: Stones on the Table
statement: Given n and a color string of length n, count stones to remove so neighboring stones have different colors.
input: First line n. Second line string of length n.

title: Wrong Subtraction
statement: Given n and k. Repeat k times: if n ends with 0, divide by 10, else subtract 1. Output result.

title: Word Capitalization
statement: Capitalize the first character of the word, leave the rest unchanged.
output: The word with the first character capitalized.
